channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Who is Trevor Milton, the startup founder and donor pardoned by Trump
item.description: Milton said Thursday in a post on social platform X that he spoke to Trump over the phone and that he signed his pardon of “innocence.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House may change how journalists can ask Trump questions
item.description: The White House is currently locked in a legal battle with the Associated Press over its decision to ban the wire service from key West Wing spaces over its “Gulf of America” stylebook policy.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Myanmar quake death toll rises to 1,644 as resistance movement announces partial ceasefire
item.description: A unilateral partial ceasefire to facilitate earthquake relief efforts was announced on Saturday by Myanmar’s shadow National Unity Government, which coordinates the popular struggle against the ruling military. The country's death toll from the disaster soared to 1,644.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: RECALL: Thousands of pounds of Our Family coffee mislabeled
item.description: The FDA lists this as a class II recall, in which use or exposure to a product “may cause temporary or medically reversible adverse health consequences or where the probability of serious adverse health consequences is remote.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, ABC 4

item.title: What to do before you quit your job
item.description: Thinking about changing jobs? You’re in good company. Job tenure is now the shortest it has been in over 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Rosie Cima, NewsNation

item.title: Protesters rebelling against Elon Musk's purge of US government swarm Tesla showrooms
item.description: Crowds protesting billionaire Elon Musk’s purge of the U.S. government under President Donald Trump began amassing outside Tesla dealerships throughout the U.S. and in some cities in Europe on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Disney failed to protect guests from employee taking ‘upskirt’ photos, lawsuit alleges
item.description: A new lawsuit alleges that Disney failed to protect guests from employee taking "upskirt" photos of guests at its Florida theme park in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Passenger flight and Air Force jet diverted from potential collision at DC airport
item.description: A U.S. passenger flight preparing to leave the nation's capital and an incoming military jet received instructions to divert and prevent a possible collision, officials said. Delta Air Lines Flight 2983 was cleared for takeoff at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport on Friday around 3:15 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge rules Trump administration cannot immediately deport Tufts student
item.description: A federal judge recently ruled a Tufts University grad student cannot be immediately deported. Meanwhile, ICE agents made their latest student detainment near the University of Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baby's corpse, busted casket found on grounds of West Virginia cemetery
item.description: A baby's corpse and a busted casket were found at a cemetery in Salt Rock, West Virginia, on Thursday evening, according to the Cabell County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Barren, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Truck hauling over 125 cows rolls over in Northwest Indiana
item.description: LAKE COUNTY, Ind. — Two cows were injured after a truck hauling dairy cattle overturned in northwest Indiana during rush hour on Friday morning. According to Officials from the Gary Fire Department, the crash unfolded just before 8 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell warned Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ asks Supreme Court to intervene in deportation flights case
item.description: The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court on Friday to allow it to invoke the Alien Enemies Act to carry out swift deportations.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Trump holds 'productive call' with new Canadian prime minister
item.description: President Trump spoke with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Friday for the first time since Carney was elected earlier in the month amid tensions between the two nations.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Musk to travel to Wisconsin ahead of judicial race, hand out $1M checks
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk announced late Thursday he would be traveling to Wisconsin ahead of a high-stakes state Supreme Court race to talk with voters and hand out $1 million checks to several people who have already cast ballots.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration moves to end union rights for many federal workers
item.description: The order targets agencies it says have a national security mission but many of the departments don’t have a strict national security connection.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Americans’ outlook on economy becomes a bit more pessimistic: Poll
item.description: Around 57 percent of those earning under $50,000 said their financial situation is “bad,” while 39 percent argued it is “good,” the poll found.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: How this auto union leader's support for Trump's tariffs scrambled labor politics again
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has once again scrambled the politics of organized labor and the working class with his planned tariffs on auto imports.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Yolanda Saldívar, killer of Selena, denied parole
item.description: The woman convicted of killing Tejano music legend Selena Quintanilla-Perez has been denied parole after spending decades behind bars for fatally shooting the young singer at a Texas motel in 1995, the state’s parole board announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge: High heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge found the extreme heat in Texas prisons is “plainly unconstitutional” but did not order the state to begin installing air conditioners.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Congress questions the FAA, US Army and NTSB over deadly midair collision in DC
item.description: The head of the National Transportation Safety Board appeared alongside officials from the Federal Aviation Administration and the Army's aviation division at a hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, KRON4

item.title: First job? Some tax tips for first-time filers
item.description: The deadline to file your taxes is less than a month away, and if you're doing them for the first time, you might be feeling added pressure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bestselling author of 'The Vampire Diaries' dies at 66
item.description: Bestselling author L.J. Smith, best known for the book series "The Vampire Diaries," which also became a TV hit, has died at 66.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Stone, Fox 5

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University detained, video shows masked people handcuffing her
item.description: SOMERVILLE, Mass. (AP) — A Turkish national and doctoral student at Tufts University has been detained by U.S. Department of Homeland Security agents without explanation, her lawyer said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, KATHY McCORMACK and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pilot rescued with 2 girls from icy Alaska lake was not authorized to have passengers, official says
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — A pilot rescued with two young family members after they survived a night on the wing of an airplane partially submerged in an Alaska lake is a student pilot who was not authorized to fly with passengers, according to a U.S. official and federal aviation records.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Million-dollar lottery ticket was nearly thrown out, winner says
item.description: “I was actually kind of going to throw them away, because I said ‘I’ve never won the lottery,’” said Joaquin Vaughn Redo.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-North Dakota lawmaker gets 10 years for going to Europe with intent to pay for sex with a minor
item.description: A once-powerful former North Dakota lawmaker was sentenced Wednesday to 10 years in prison for traveling to Europe with the intent to pay for sex with a minor.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A heart-shaped note was found in socks bound for Luigi Mangione
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Someone tucked a heart-shaped note of encouragement into socks packed for Luigi Mangione to wear to court last month in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case, according to court documents made public Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chewing gum releases thousands of microplastics into body, study finds
item.description: A recent study found that chewing gum can release up to thousands of microplastics per piece and potentially be ingested, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, ABC 4

item.title: Cartel 'sicarios' crossed into US soil in murder-for-hire scheme, prosecutors say
item.description: A Mexican cartel leader has been sentenced to U.S. federal prison for a murder-for-hire scheme in which prosecutors say he brought sicarios, or hit men, across the border into Laredo, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: NTSB chairwoman says reviewing the data after midair crash may prevent the next aviation accident
item.description: Someone should have spotted the alarming number of near misses in the skies over the nation's capital before the fatal midair collision that killed 67 people in January, and reviewing the data now could prevent future crashes, according to the head of the agency investigating the crash.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court in a 2-1 decision Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from swiftly deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act. The Justice Department had urged the three-judge panel on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Education Department restores IDR student loan applications
item.description: The applications were put on pause after a federal appeals court ruled the Biden administration’s Saving on Valuable Education (SAVE) income-drive repayment (IDR) plan was illegal.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: San Diego passes first-in-the-nation policy to ban digital-only coupons at grocery stores
item.description: The San Diego City Council has passed the Grocery Pricing Transparency Ordinance, requiring grocers to offer both digital and paper coupons to all customers, promoting equal access to grocery savings opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A new Chili’s near Scranton will be a throwback to 'The Office,' 'awesome blossom' and all
item.description: It has been nearly two decades since the workplace mockumentary "The Office” first set an episode inside a Chili's, where Michael Scott handed out Dundie awards to his ever-tolerant employees, including trophies for the whitest sneakers and for stinking up the bathroom.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4
